metadata.author: Claude Code + petteri
metadata.purpose: End-to-end testing plan for the full MinIVess MLOps pipeline

key-design-decisions.decision: All 5 local-GPU models: DynUNet, SAM3 Vanilla, SAM3 Hybrid, CoMMA Mamba, U-Like Mamba
Training: 2 epochs, 3 folds per model via debug_all_models.yaml
Champion metric: val_compound_masd_cldice = 0.5*(1 - MASD/50) + 0.5*clDice
Post-training: SWA + calibration + conformal prediction plugins
Ensembles: per-model best fold + cross-model ensemble
External datasets: DeepVess + TubeNet only (VesselNN EXCLUDED)
Deployment: champion by compound metric to ONNX to BentoML serve
BentoML response: mask + calibrated probabilities + uncertainty intervals
Monitoring: Grafana dashboards (provisioned JSON), Evidently drift, Prometheus scraping BentoML
Infrastructure: pytest-docker plugin managing real docker-compose files
Execution: Docker via Prefect (real containerized, not host Python)
Verification: Triple: Prefect API status + MLflow artifacts + container logs
MLflow storage: verify artifacts in MinIO + full round-trip retrieval
Logging: structured JSONL events for flow/task/agent transitions
Test location: make test-e2e with @slow + @integration markers
Runtime: ~40~60 min accepted, marked @slow
Annotation flow: no-op (empty DVC queue OK) + BentoML health check
Data flow: verify MiniVess + Pandera/GE validation + download DeepVess/TubeNet
Biostatistics: Markdown comparison table + DuckDB Parquet export
Drift simulation: deferred to P0 issue #574

phase.name: Fix Broken Inter-Flow Seams
phase.description:
The audit found that train flow never logs checkpoint_dir tags to MLflow. Post-training gets 0 checkpoints, analysis evaluates 0 models. Everything downstream is empty. This MUST be fixed before any e2e test can pass.
Root causes (from prefect-flow-connectivity.md): 1. train_flow.py never logs checkpoint_dir_fold_N to MLflow tags 2. Post-training receives empty checkpoint list from FlowContract 3. analysis_flow.py hardcodes experiment_name="minivess_training" (breaks _DEBUG suffix) 4. No MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX propagation through flow chain 5. No structured JSONL logging for flow/task transitions
Cross-reference: docs/planning/prefect-flow-connectivity-execution-plan.xml contains the detailed fix plan. Phase 0 here ensures those fixes are in place and verified before e2e testing begins.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Train flow logs checkpoint_dir_fold_N tags for each fold in MLflow
Train flow logs parent_run_id and flow_status=FLOW_COMPLETE tags on completion
FlowContract.find_fold_checkpoints() returns non-empty list for a real training run
Post-training plugins receive non-empty checkpoint paths
Analysis flow uses resolve_experiment_name(), not hardcoded experiment names
MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX propagates through all flow containers
Structured JSONL events logged for flow start/end transitions

task.name: Train flow: log checkpoint_dir and parent_run_id tags
task.description:
After each fold's training completes in train_flow.py, log MLflow tags: - mlflow.set_tag("checkpoint_dir_fold_{n}", str(checkpoint_dir)) - mlflow.set_tag("parent_run_id", parent_run_id) On parent run completion: - mlflow.set_tag("flow_status", "FLOW_COMPLETE")
This is the root cause of the entire downstream pipeline failure. Without these tags, FlowContract.find_fold_checkpoints() returns [], post-training plugins skip, and analysis evaluates nothing.

test-cases.case: test_fold_training_logs_checkpoint_dir_tag: After a fold completes, verify mlflow tag checkpoint_dir_fold_0 exists and points to a real path
test_fold_training_logs_parent_run_id_tag: Verify parent_run_id tag matches the outer run
test_parent_run_completion_logs_flow_complete: After all folds, verify flow_status=FLOW_COMPLETE tag
test_multiple_folds_log_separate_checkpoint_tags: With 3 folds, verify checkpoint_dir_fold_0, _fold_1, _fold_2 all present
test_checkpoint_dir_tag_value_is_absolute_path: Tag value must be an absolute path, not relative

task.name: Flow contract: verify find_fold_checkpoints with real tags
task.description:
Verify FlowContract.find_fold_checkpoints(parent_run_id) correctly reads checkpoint_dir_fold_N tags from MLflow and returns structured results. Use a real local MLflow file store (tmp_path based), not mocks.
This method already exists in flow_contract.py but has never been tested against real MLflow data. We need integration-level verification that the tag format matches what train_flow.py writes (from T0.1).

test-cases.case: test_find_fold_checkpoints_returns_sorted_folds: Write 3 checkpoint tags, verify list sorted by fold_id
test_find_fold_checkpoints_empty_when_no_tags: Run with no checkpoint tags, verify returns []
test_find_fold_checkpoints_returns_path_objects: Verify each result has checkpoint_dir as pathlib.Path
test_find_fold_checkpoints_ignores_non_checkpoint_tags: Write non-checkpoint tags, verify they are filtered out
test_find_fold_checkpoints_with_invalid_run_id: Pass a nonexistent run_id, verify returns [] (no crash)

task.name: Post-training flow: consume checkpoint paths from FlowContract
task.description:
Modify post_training_flow.py to call FlowContract.find_fold_checkpoints(parent_run_id) instead of using checkpoint_paths=[] default. This ensures SWA, calibration, and conformal plugins receive non-empty checkpoint lists.
Current behavior: post_training_flow.py calls find_upstream_safely() which returns a run_id but no checkpoint paths. The PluginInput.checkpoint_paths defaults to [] and all plugins skip with "No checkpoint paths provided".
Target behavior: FlowContract reads checkpoint_dir_fold_N tags from the upstream training run and populates PluginInput.checkpoint_paths with real paths.

test-cases.case: test_post_training_reads_checkpoints_from_flow_contract: Mock FlowContract to return 3 folds, verify PluginInput.checkpoint_paths has 3 entries
test_post_training_fails_gracefully_when_no_checkpoints: FlowContract returns [], verify plugins skip with warning (not crash)
test_swa_plugin_receives_non_empty_checkpoints: Verify SWA plugin gets real checkpoint paths from FlowContract
test_calibration_plugin_receives_non_empty_checkpoints: Verify calibration plugin gets checkpoint paths

task.name: Analysis flow: use resolve_experiment_name for debug isolation
task.description:
Replace any hardcoded experiment names in analysis_flow.py with FlowContract._resolve_experiment() or the standalone resolve_experiment_name() from constants.py. This ensures debug runs (MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX=_DEBUG) search the correct experiment namespace.
Current bug: analysis_flow.py searches experiment_name="minivess_training" but debug training runs create experiment "minivess_training_DEBUG". Analysis finds 0 runs and produces no outputs.

test-cases.case: test_analysis_resolves_experiment_with_debug_suffix: Set MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX=_DEBUG, verify analysis searches "minivess_training_DEBUG"
test_analysis_resolves_experiment_without_suffix: Unset MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX, verify analysis searches "minivess_training"
test_resolve_experiment_name_in_constants: Verify resolve_experiment_name() applies suffix from env var

task.name: Debug suffix propagation in compose and scripts
task.description:
Ensure MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX is exported in debug runs (scripts/run_debug.sh) and passed through docker-compose.flows.yml environment blocks. All flows must see the same suffix so inter-flow MLflow queries work.
Verify that docker-compose.flows.yml includes MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX in the x-common-env anchor (or equivalent environment section).

test-cases.case: test_debug_suffix_in_compose_env: Parse docker-compose.flows.yml YAML, verify MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX present in x-common-env or environment
test_run_debug_sh_exports_suffix: Read run_debug.sh, verify it exports MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX=_DEBUG

task.name: Structured JSONL logging for flow/task transitions
task.description:
Create src/minivess/observability/structured_logging.py with JSONL event emitters for flow lifecycle events. Each event is a single JSON line with: - timestamp (ISO 8601 UTC) - event_type (flow_start, flow_end, task_start, task_end, agent_decision) - flow_name, task_name (where applicable) - status (started, completed, failed, skipped) - metadata (dict with additional context)
Integrate with Prefect's logger so events appear in Prefect UI logs. Write to a JSONL file at LOGS_DIR/flow_events.jsonl for container log parsing.
This supports Phase 2 verification (T2.3: container log verification).

test-cases.case: test_flow_start_event_has_required_fields: Emit flow_start, verify JSON has timestamp, event_type, flow_name, status
test_flow_end_event_includes_duration: Emit flow_end, verify duration_seconds field present
test_task_start_event_includes_task_name: Emit task_start, verify task_name field
test_jsonl_output_is_parseable: Write multiple events, verify each line is valid JSON via json.loads()
test_event_timestamp_is_utc_iso8601: Verify timestamp matches ISO 8601 format with UTC timezone
test_events_written_to_file_at_logs_dir: Verify events are appended to LOGS_DIR/flow_events.jsonl
test_prefect_logger_integration: Verify events emitted via get_run_logger() when Prefect is active

phase.name: Individual Flow Testing
phase.description:
Each flow tested in isolation within its Docker container. Upstream data is either real (for early flows) or mocked/pre-seeded in MLflow (for downstream flows). Each test verifies the flow produces correct MLflow artifacts, tags, and metrics.
All tests run in Docker via Prefect (no MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1). Tests use pytest-docker fixtures from Phase 4 infrastructure.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Acquisition flow downloads MiniVess + DeepVess + TubeNet and logs to MLflow
Data flow passes Pandera/GE validation and logs splits_path tag
Annotation flow handles empty DVC queue gracefully + BentoML health check
Training flow produces checkpoints for all 5 models x 3 folds x 2 epochs
Post-training flow executes SWA + calibration plugins with real checkpoints
Analysis flow evaluates models, creates ensembles, tags champion
Biostatistics flow generates comparison table + DuckDB/Parquet exports
Deploy flow exports champion to ONNX and serves via BentoML

task.name: Acquisition flow: verify MiniVess + download external datasets
task.description:
Test the acquisition flow in its Docker container: 1. Verify MiniVess volumes exist in the data volume mount (/app/data/raw/minivess) 2. Download DeepVess dataset (Cornell eCommons) 3. Download TubeNet 2PM dataset (UCL Figshare) 4. Do NOT download VesselNN (excluded: potential MiniVess data leakage, PI authored it) 5. Log acquisition_complete tag to MLflow 6. Verify volumes are accessible and converted to NIfTI format
Uses: src/minivess/orchestration/flows/acquisition_flow.py Config: AcquisitionConfig with datasets=[minivess, deepvess, tubenet_2pm]

test-cases.case: test_minivess_volumes_accessible: Verify /app/data/raw/minivess contains NIfTI volumes
test_deepvess_download_succeeds: Verify DeepVess dataset downloaded and accessible
test_tubenet_download_succeeds: Verify TubeNet 2PM dataset downloaded and accessible
test_vesselnn_not_downloaded: Verify VesselNN is NOT in downloaded datasets (excluded)
test_acquisition_complete_mlflow_tag: Verify acquisition_complete tag logged to MLflow
test_structured_jsonl_events_emitted: Verify flow_start and flow_end JSONL events in logs

task.name: Data engineering flow: Pandera/GE validation + splits
task.description:
Test the data engineering flow in isolation: 1. Run Pandera schema validation on MiniVess volume metadata (spacing, shape, dtype) 2. Run Great Expectations validation suite (non-null, range checks, consistency) 3. Verify splits file (configs/splits/3fold_seed42.json) is valid and consistent 4. Log data_flow_complete tag with splits_path to MLflow 5. Verify debug suffix propagation on experiment name
Uses: src/minivess/orchestration/flows/data_flow.py

test-cases.case: test_pandera_validation_passes: Verify MiniVess volume metadata passes Pandera schema
test_great_expectations_suite_passes: Verify GE batch validation produces no critical failures
test_splits_file_consistent: Verify 3fold_seed42.json has 47 train + 23 val volumes per fold
test_data_flow_complete_tag_logged: Verify data_flow_complete + splits_path tags in MLflow
test_debug_suffix_on_experiment_name: With _DEBUG suffix, verify experiment uses correct name

task.name: Annotation flow: empty queue no-op + BentoML health check
task.description:
Test the annotation flow handles the empty-queue case gracefully: 1. Check DVC for un-versioned volumes. In e2e test scenario, finds none. 2. Return {status: "ok", new_volumes: 0} without error 3. Verify BentoML pre-segmentation endpoint is reachable (HTTP health check) 4. Log annotation_complete tag to MLflow
Uses: src/minivess/orchestration/flows/annotation_flow.py

test-cases.case: test_empty_dvc_queue_returns_ok: No new volumes, verify flow returns status=ok, new_volumes=0
test_bentoml_health_check_reachable: Verify BentoML endpoint responds 200 on /healthz
test_annotation_complete_tag_logged: Verify annotation_complete tag in MLflow
test_no_error_on_empty_queue: Verify no ERROR level log lines when queue is empty

task.name: Training flow: all 5 models x 2 epochs x 3 folds
task.description:
Test the training flow with debug_all_models.yaml config: - Models: dynunet, sam3_vanilla, sam3_hybrid, comma_mamba, ulike_mamba - max_epochs: 2, num_folds: 3, max_train_volumes: 2, max_val_volumes: 2 - Loss: cbdice_cldice (default) - Verify checkpoint files exist after each fold (non-empty .pt files) - Verify MLflow tags: checkpoint_dir_fold_N, parent_run_id, flow_status=FLOW_COMPLETE - Verify metrics logged: train_loss, val_dice per epoch
Duration: ~30 min for 5 models x 3 folds x 2 epochs on local GPU. SAM3 Hybrid requires patch_size=[32,32,3] on 8GB GPU.
Uses: src/minivess/orchestration/flows/train_flow.py Config: configs/experiment/debug_all_models.yaml

test-cases.case: test_dynunet_3folds_checkpoints_exist: Verify 3 checkpoint files for DynUNet after training
test_sam3_vanilla_3folds_checkpoints_exist: Verify 3 checkpoint files for SAM3 Vanilla
test_sam3_hybrid_3folds_checkpoints_exist: Verify 3 checkpoint files for SAM3 Hybrid (patch 32x32x3)
test_comma_mamba_3folds_checkpoints_exist: Verify 3 checkpoint files for CoMMA Mamba
test_ulike_mamba_3folds_checkpoints_exist: Verify 3 checkpoint files for U-Like Mamba
test_checkpoint_dir_fold_tags_in_mlflow: For each model, verify checkpoint_dir_fold_0/1/2 tags
test_parent_run_id_tag_present: Verify parent_run_id tag on each model's parent run
test_flow_status_complete_tag: Verify flow_status=FLOW_COMPLETE on parent runs
test_train_loss_metrics_logged: Verify train_loss metric exists for each epoch
test_val_dice_metrics_logged: Verify val_dice metric logged at validation intervals
test_checkpoint_files_non_empty: Each .pt file must be > 0 bytes

task.name: Post-training flow: SWA + calibration + conformal
task.description:
Test the post-training flow consuming checkpoints from T1.4: 1. Call FlowContract.find_fold_checkpoints() to get checkpoint paths from training 2. Run SWA plugin: average weights across folds, produce swa checkpoint 3. Run calibration plugin: temperature scaling on validation set, log temperature param 4. Run conformal prediction plugin: compute conformal prediction sets 5. Verify sibling runs (__swa, __calibrated, __conformal) created in MLflow 6. Verify each sibling run has appropriate artifacts
Note: conformal prediction may not be in debug_all_models.yaml plugins list. The config specifies plugins: [swa, calibration]. Test what is configured.
Uses: src/minivess/orchestration/flows/post_training_flow.py Upstream: T1.4 training runs

test-cases.case: test_post_training_finds_upstream_checkpoints: Verify FlowContract returns non-empty checkpoint list from training run
test_swa_sibling_run_created: Verify MLflow run with __swa suffix exists after post-training
test_calibration_sibling_run_created: Verify MLflow run with __calibrated suffix exists
test_calibrated_model_has_temperature_param: Verify temperature parameter logged in calibration run
test_swa_checkpoint_artifact_exists: Verify SWA averaged checkpoint file in MLflow artifacts
test_plugin_skips_logged_not_crashed: If a plugin has no checkpoint, verify warning log (not exception)
test_post_training_flow_status_complete: Verify flow_status=FLOW_COMPLETE on post-training parent run

task.name: Analysis flow: evaluation + ensembles + champion tagging
task.description:
Test the analysis flow end-to-end: 1. Evaluate all 5 models from T1.4 + post-training variants from T1.5 2. Compute per-model metrics: DSC, clDice, MASD, HD95 3. Compute val_compound_masd_cldice = 0.5*(1 - MASD/50) + 0.5*clDice 4. Create per-model best-fold ensembles (select best fold per model) 5. Create cross-model ensemble (combine best models) 6. Tag champion by val_compound_masd_cldice (MetricsReloaded) 7. Generate comparison table (Markdown + LaTeX) 8. Generate analysis figures (PNG + SVG)
Uses: src/minivess/orchestration/flows/analysis_flow.py Upstream: T1.4 training + T1.5 post-training

test-cases.case: test_all_5_models_evaluated: Verify analysis evaluated at least dynunet + sam3_vanilla + sam3_hybrid + comma_mamba + ulike_mamba
test_champion_tagged_by_compound_metric: Verify champion model tagged in MLflow with val_compound_masd_cldice
test_compound_metric_formula_correct: Verify val_compound_masd_cldice = 0.5*(1 - MASD/50) + 0.5*clDice
test_per_model_best_fold_ensemble_created: Verify per-model ensemble artifacts in MLflow
test_cross_model_ensemble_created: Verify cross-model ensemble artifact exists
test_comparison_table_markdown_generated: Verify comparison table .md file in outputs/
test_comparison_table_has_all_models: Verify Markdown table contains all 5 model names
test_analysis_figures_generated: Verify at least 1 PNG figure artifact in MLflow or outputs/
test_analysis_uses_resolved_experiment_name: Verify experiment name includes _DEBUG suffix if set

task.name: Biostatistics flow: summary + DuckDB + Parquet
task.description:
Test the biostatistics flow: 1. Read all analysis results from MLflow experiment runs 2. Generate Markdown comparison table across all models and run names 3. Export to DuckDB database + Parquet files 4. Verify DuckDB is queryable (SELECT * FROM runs) 5. Verify Parquet files readable via pyarrow/pandas
Uses: src/minivess/orchestration/flows/biostatistics_flow.py Upstream: T1.6 analysis results in MLflow

test-cases.case: test_markdown_comparison_table_generated: Verify .md file exists and is non-empty
test_markdown_table_contains_all_models: Verify table has rows for all 5 models
test_duckdb_export_created: Verify .duckdb file exists (if not gitignored in output path)
test_duckdb_queryable: Open DuckDB file, execute SELECT COUNT(*) FROM runs, verify > 0
test_parquet_export_created: Verify .parquet file exists
test_parquet_readable: Read Parquet with pyarrow, verify columns include model_name, val_dice
test_biostatistics_flow_complete_tag: Verify flow_status=FLOW_COMPLETE tag in MLflow

task.name: Deployment flow: champion to ONNX to BentoML
task.description:
Test the deployment flow end-to-end: 1. Discover champion model from MLflow (tagged by analysis flow in T1.6) 2. Load champion checkpoint and export to ONNX format 3. Verify ONNX file is valid (onnx.load + onnx.checker.check_model) 4. Import ONNX model into BentoML model store 5. Start BentoML serving endpoint 6. Verify health check endpoint responds with HTTP 200
Champion selection uses val_compound_masd_cldice metric. ONNX export must handle all 5 model architectures via ModelAdapter.export_onnx().
Uses: src/minivess/orchestration/flows/deploy_flow.py Upstream: T1.6 champion tag in MLflow

test-cases.case: test_champion_discovered_from_mlflow: Verify deploy flow finds a champion model with the compound metric tag
test_onnx_export_produces_valid_file: Verify ONNX file passes onnx.checker.check_model()
test_onnx_file_non_empty: Verify ONNX file size > 0 bytes
test_bentoml_model_import: Verify model imported into BentoML store (bentoml.models.list() includes it)
test_bentoml_health_check: Verify BentoML /healthz endpoint returns HTTP 200
test_deploy_flow_complete_tag: Verify flow_status=FLOW_COMPLETE tag in MLflow

phase.name: Integration Contracts
phase.description: Verify the inter-flow communication contracts work end-to-end. Triple verification: Prefect API status + MLflow artifacts + container logs. These tests run AFTER Phase 1 flows complete and verify the seams.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: MLflow artifacts stored in MinIO (not local filesystem), full round-trip verified
All Prefect flow runs show COMPLETED status via Prefect API
Container logs contain structured JSONL events, no unexpected errors
All volume mounts contain expected artifacts after pipeline completion

task.name: MLflow contract: verify artifacts in MinIO with round-trip
task.description: After the full pipeline completes, verify: 1. Training artifacts (checkpoints, configs) exist in MinIO bucket via boto3 - Bucket: mlflow-artifacts (from .env.example MLFLOW_ARTIFACT_BUCKET) - Access: MinIO at minio:9000 (MINIO_DOCKER_HOST:MINIO_API_PORT) 2. Download artifacts back via MLflow API (mlflow.artifacts.download_artifacts) 3. Verify downloaded artifacts match uploaded ones (file size, content hash) 4. Verify experiment names use debug suffix correctly 5. Verify all runs have FINISHED status (not RUNNING or FAILED)

test-cases.case: test_training_artifacts_in_minio: boto3 list_objects on mlflow-artifacts bucket returns training checkpoint keys
test_mlflow_download_matches_upload: Download checkpoint via MLflow API, verify SHA256 matches original
test_experiment_names_have_debug_suffix: All experiments created during e2e use _DEBUG suffix
test_all_runs_finished_status: Query MLflow for all runs, verify none have status RUNNING or FAILED
test_config_artifact_round_trip: Upload resolved_config.yaml, download, verify YAML content matches

task.name: Prefect contract: verify flow run status via API
task.description:
Query the Prefect API for all flow runs created during e2e testing: 1. List all flow runs via Prefect client (prefect.client.get_client) 2. Verify each flow run has state_type=COMPLETED 3. Verify no FAILED, CRASHED, or CANCELLED flow runs 4. Verify task-level transitions are recorded 5. Cross-reference Prefect flow names with expected flows: acquisition, data, annotation, train, post_training, analysis, biostatistics, deploy
Prefect API at: http://PREFECT_DOCKER_HOST:PREFECT_PORT/api

test-cases.case: test_all_expected_flows_completed: Verify 8 flow types show COMPLETED state
test_no_failed_flow_runs: Verify zero FAILED or CRASHED flow runs
test_task_runs_recorded: Verify task-level runs exist for each flow
test_flow_run_duration_reasonable: Verify no flow run exceeds 60 min (indicates hang)
test_prefect_api_healthy: Verify Prefect API /health returns 200

task.name: Container log verification: JSONL events + no errors
task.description:
Capture and analyze container logs for each flow run: 1. Read JSONL events from LOGS_DIR/flow_events.jsonl (written by T0.6) 2. Verify each flow has at least flow_start + flow_end events 3. Verify no ERROR-level log lines (warnings OK if suppressed per Rule #7) 4. Verify Pydantic AI agent events logged if any agent invocations occurred 5. Parse JSONL events with json.loads() (no regex, Rule #16)
Container logs captured via docker logs or pytest-docker log fixture.

test-cases.case: test_jsonl_events_file_exists: Verify flow_events.jsonl exists in logs volume
test_each_flow_has_start_and_end_events: For each flow name, verify both flow_start and flow_end events
test_no_error_level_in_container_logs: Parse container logs, verify no ERROR lines (except suppressed warnings)
test_jsonl_events_parseable: Every line in flow_events.jsonl passes json.loads()
test_flow_end_events_have_status_completed: All flow_end events have status="completed"

task.name: Volume mount verification: all artifact paths populated
task.description:
After the full pipeline completes, verify all Docker volume mounts contain the expected artifacts. This catches silent failures where a flow "completes" but writes nothing to disk.
Volume mounts (from deployment/CLAUDE.md): - checkpoint_cache:/app/checkpoints: model .pt files (one per fold per model) - mlruns_data:/app/mlruns: MLflow experiment data - data_cache:/app/data: preprocessed volumes - configs_splits:/app/configs/splits: fold definition JSON - logs_data:/app/logs: JSONL events, training CSVs
Each volume must contain non-empty files matching expected patterns.

test-cases.case: test_checkpoint_cache_has_model_files: Verify .pt files exist in checkpoint volume for each trained model
test_mlruns_data_has_experiments: Verify MLflow experiment directories exist in mlruns volume
test_data_cache_has_preprocessed_volumes: Verify data cache contains processed NIfTI files
test_configs_splits_has_fold_json: Verify 3fold_seed42.json exists in splits volume
test_logs_data_has_jsonl_events: Verify flow_events.jsonl exists in logs volume
test_no_empty_volumes: Verify each volume has at least one file (catch silent write failures)

phase.name: Monitoring and Serving
phase.description:
Verify the full monitoring stack: BentoML inference with calibrated responses, Grafana dashboard provisioning, Evidently data quality/drift reports, and Prometheus metrics pipeline. These tests verify production-readiness of the serving and observability infrastructure.
Drift SIMULATION is deferred to issue #574. This phase only verifies the monitoring infrastructure works (Evidently report generation, Prometheus scraping, Grafana dashboard rendering) using real inference data.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: BentoML returns mask + calibrated probabilities + uncertainty intervals
Grafana has 4 provisioned dashboards with functional panels
Evidently generates data quality + prediction drift HTML reports
Prometheus scrapes BentoML metrics and Grafana queries them

task.name: BentoML inference with calibrated response
task.description:
Send a real external test volume (DeepVess or TubeNet) to the BentoML endpoint and verify the response contains all three outputs:
1. mask: binary segmentation mask (same spatial dims as input volume) 2. probabilities: calibrated probability map (post temperature scaling from T1.5) 3. uncertainties: dict with conformal prediction intervals
Verify: - Response HTTP status 200 - mask dtype is uint8 or bool, shape matches input spatial dims - probabilities are float32, values in [0, 1] - uncertainties dict has expected keys (lower_bound, upper_bound, coverage) - Inference latency under 30 seconds for a single volume

test-cases.case: test_inference_returns_mask: Send volume, verify response["mask"] exists with correct spatial dims
test_mask_dtype_binary: Verify mask values are 0 or 1 (uint8 or bool)
test_inference_returns_calibrated_probabilities: Verify response["probabilities"] exists, dtype float32
test_probabilities_in_unit_interval: Verify all probability values in [0.0, 1.0]
test_inference_returns_uncertainties: Verify response["uncertainties"] dict has lower_bound, upper_bound
test_inference_latency_under_30s: Verify end-to-end inference completes within 30 seconds
test_inference_on_deepvess_volume: Send a real DeepVess volume, verify non-trivial mask (not all zeros)

task.name: Grafana dashboard provisioning
task.description:
Create provisioned Grafana dashboard JSON files and configure automatic loading:
1. deployment/grafana/dashboards/model-performance.json - Panels: val_dice, val_cldice, val_masd per model (bar chart + time series) 2. deployment/grafana/dashboards/inference-latency.json - Panels: BentoML request duration histogram, p50/p95/p99 latencies 3. deployment/grafana/dashboards/data-drift.json - Panels: Evidently drift score time series, feature importance 4. deployment/grafana/dashboards/bentoml-requests.json - Panels: request rate, error rate, active connections
Configure Grafana provisioning in docker-compose.yml: - deployment/grafana/provisioning/dashboards/dashboards.yml - deployment/grafana/provisioning/datasources/datasources.yml (Prometheus)
Test via Grafana API: /api/dashboards returns 4 dashboards, each has panels.

test-cases.case: test_grafana_api_healthy: Verify GET /api/health returns "ok"
test_four_dashboards_provisioned: Verify GET /api/search returns 4 dashboards
test_model_performance_dashboard_has_panels: Verify model-performance dashboard has val_dice panel
test_inference_latency_dashboard_has_panels: Verify inference-latency dashboard has histogram panel
test_data_drift_dashboard_has_panels: Verify data-drift dashboard has drift score panel
test_bentoml_requests_dashboard_has_panels: Verify bentoml-requests dashboard has request rate panel
test_prometheus_datasource_configured: Verify Grafana has Prometheus datasource via /api/datasources

task.name: Evidently data quality + drift report generation
task.description:
Wire Evidently into the BentoML serving pipeline to generate quality and drift reports after inference on external test volumes:
1. Data quality report: input feature statistics (intensity distribution, spatial dimensions, spacing range) 2. Prediction drift report: output distribution compared to training set predictions (proxy for model drift detection) 3. Save reports as HTML artifacts in MLflow under the deploy experiment
NOTE: Full drift SIMULATION (synthetic data shift, concept drift, model degradation) is deferred to issue #574 (P0). This task only verifies that the Evidently infrastructure works and can generate reports.

test-cases.case: test_data_quality_report_generated: Verify Evidently DataQualityReport produces non-empty HTML
test_prediction_drift_report_generated: Verify Evidently DataDriftReport produces non-empty HTML
test_reports_saved_as_mlflow_artifacts: Verify HTML reports uploaded to MLflow artifact store
test_report_html_contains_expected_sections: Verify HTML has data quality metrics sections
test_evidently_handles_3d_volume_features: Verify Evidently works with 3D spatial statistics

task.name: Prometheus to Grafana metrics pipeline
task.description:
Configure the full metrics pipeline: BentoML exposes metrics to Prometheus to Grafana.
1. Configure BentoML to expose /metrics endpoint (Prometheus format) - Request count, request duration, error count, active requests 2. Configure Prometheus to scrape BentoML metrics - deployment/prometheus/prometheus.yml with bentoml scrape target 3. Verify metrics flow: BentoML /metrics to Prometheus /api/v1/query to Grafana panels 4. After running inference (T3.1), verify Prometheus has bentoml_* metrics 5. Verify Grafana can query these metrics (datasource test)
Prometheus config at: deployment/prometheus/prometheus.yml BentoML at: BENTOML_DOCKER_HOST:BENTOML_PORT (minivess-bento:3333)

test-cases.case: test_bentoml_metrics_endpoint_accessible: Verify GET /metrics on BentoML returns Prometheus-format text
test_prometheus_scrapes_bentoml: Query Prometheus /api/v1/targets, verify BentoML target is UP
test_prometheus_has_bentoml_metrics: Query Prometheus for bentoml_api_server_request_total, verify non-empty
test_grafana_prometheus_datasource_healthy: Verify Grafana datasource health check passes
test_grafana_query_returns_data: Execute a Grafana Prometheus query, verify non-empty result

phase.name: E2E Pytest Harness
phase.description:
Build the pytest infrastructure that orchestrates all previous phases into a single make test-e2e invocation. Uses pytest-docker to manage the real docker-compose stack (infrastructure + flow containers).
Runtime: ~40~60 min total (training is the bottleneck). Markers: @pytest.mark.slow + @pytest.mark.integration Execution: Docker via Prefect (no MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1 escape hatch).

acceptance-criteria.criterion: pytest-docker manages real docker-compose.yml + docker-compose.flows.yml
Health check fixtures wait for PostgreSQL, MinIO, MLflow, Prefect, Grafana
Full pipeline passes end-to-end in a single make test-e2e invocation
Makefile has test-e2e target with 1-hour timeout
Issue #366 updated with external GPU verification plan

task.name: pytest-docker integration setup
task.description:
Add pytest-docker to dev dependencies (uv add --dev pytest-docker) and configure it to use the real docker-compose files: - deployment/docker-compose.yml (infrastructure services) - deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml (flow containers)
Create docker_compose_file fixture pointing to both compose files. The fixture must: 1. Create/use the minivess-network Docker network 2. Start infrastructure services (PostgreSQL, MinIO, MLflow, Prefect, Grafana) 3. Wait for health checks before yielding 4. Tear down after all e2e tests complete
Health checks needed: - PostgreSQL: pg_isready - MinIO: /minio/health/live - MLflow: GET /health (or /api/2.0/mlflow/experiments/list) - Prefect: GET /api/health - Grafana: GET /api/health

test-cases.case: test_postgresql_healthy: Verify PostgreSQL accepts connections via pg_isready equivalent
test_minio_healthy: Verify MinIO /minio/health/live returns 200
test_mlflow_healthy: Verify MLflow health endpoint returns 200
test_prefect_healthy: Verify Prefect /api/health returns 200
test_grafana_healthy: Verify Grafana /api/health returns "ok"
test_minivess_network_exists: Verify docker network minivess-network exists

task.name: E2E test file: full pipeline orchestration
task.description:
Create the main e2e test file that orchestrates the full pipeline: acquisition to data to annotation to train to post_training to analysis to biostatistics to deploy.
The test: 1. Starts all infrastructure via pytest-docker (T4.1 fixtures) 2. Runs each flow in Docker via docker compose run (or Prefect deployment API) 3. Verifies all Phase 1 assertions (flow outputs) 4. Verifies all Phase 2 assertions (inter-flow contracts) 5. Verifies all Phase 3 assertions (monitoring + serving) 6. Total runtime ~40~60 min, marked @slow + @integration
Flow execution order: 1. acquisition_flow (download datasets) 2. data_flow (validate + split) 3. annotation_flow (no-op, health check) 4. train_flow (5 models x 3 folds x 2 epochs) 5. post_training_flow (SWA + calibration) 6. analysis_flow (evaluate + ensemble + champion) 7. biostatistics_flow (comparison + DuckDB + Parquet) 8. deploy_flow (ONNX + BentoML)
Each flow invoked via docker compose run with proper env vars and --shm-size 8g for GPU flows. Uses --env-file for .env loading.

test-cases.case: test_full_pipeline_acquisition_to_deploy: Run all 8 flows sequentially, verify each completes without error
test_all_flows_have_completed_status_in_prefect: After pipeline, query Prefect API for COMPLETED state on all flows
test_champion_model_deployed: After pipeline, verify BentoML serves the champion model
test_mlflow_has_all_experiments: Verify MLflow has experiments for training, analysis, deploy
test_all_5_models_trained: Verify 5 model parent runs exist in MLflow training experiment
test_post_training_plugins_executed: Verify SWA + calibration sibling runs exist
test_comparison_table_generated: Verify Markdown comparison table artifact exists
test_onnx_champion_in_bentoml: Verify BentoML model store contains the champion ONNX model
test_pipeline_runtime_under_60_min: Assert total pipeline duration under 3600 seconds

task.name: Makefile: add test-e2e target
task.description:
Add test-e2e target to the Makefile that runs the full e2e test suite:
test-e2e: uv run pytest tests/v2/integration/e2e/ -x -q \ -m "slow and integration" \ --timeout=3600 \ -v
Prerequisites documented in Makefile comments: - Docker daemon running - MiniVess data downloaded (data/raw/minivess/) - .env file configured (from .env.example) - Docker images built (make build-bases + flow images) - minivess-network created (docker network create minivess-network)
Also add to the help target.

test-cases.case: test_makefile_has_test_e2e_target: Parse Makefile, verify test-e2e target exists
test_makefile_help_lists_test_e2e: Run make help, verify test-e2e appears in output
test_test_e2e_uses_correct_timeout: Verify test-e2e target has --timeout=3600

task.name: Update issue #366 with external GPU verification plan
task.description:
Update GitHub issue #366 (VesselFM + SAM3 TopoLoRA external GPU) with: 1. Cross-reference to this e2e testing plan 2. List of models deferred: VesselFM, SAM3 TopoLoRA (require > 8GB VRAM) 3. External GPU test infrastructure: RunPod / intranet server via make test-gpu 4. Cross-reference to P0 drift issue #574 5. Estimated effort and priority for external GPU testing phase
This task does NOT implement the external GPU tests, just documents the plan and links it to the e2e testing framework.

test-cases.case: Verify issue #366 updated with e2e plan cross-reference via gh issue view 366

task.name: Create pytest-docker conftest.py fixtures
task.description:
Create comprehensive conftest.py fixtures for e2e testing:
1. docker_compose_file: Returns list of compose file paths [deployment/docker-compose.yml, deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml]
2. docker_compose_project_name: Returns "e2e_test" to avoid conflicts with dev stack
3. wait_for_services: Session-scoped fixture that waits for all infra services to be healthy (PostgreSQL, MinIO, MLflow, Prefect, Grafana) with exponential backoff and 120s timeout
4. mlflow_client: Returns MlflowClient connected to test stack
5. prefect_client: Returns Prefect async client connected to test stack
6. run_flow_in_docker: Fixture function that runs a named flow via docker compose run with proper env vars, --shm-size, --env-file
7. cleanup_e2e_data: Session-scoped fixture that cleans up test experiment data after the test session (delete _DEBUG experiments)
All fixtures use .env.example values for service URLs/ports. No MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1 in e2e fixtures (Docker-only execution).

test-cases.case: test_docker_compose_file_fixture_returns_paths: Verify fixture returns 2 compose file paths that exist
test_wait_for_services_completes: Verify all 5 service health checks pass within 120s
test_mlflow_client_connects: Verify MlflowClient can list experiments
test_prefect_client_connects: Verify Prefect client can read /api/health
test_run_flow_in_docker_returns_exit_code: Run a trivial flow, verify exit code 0

cross-cutting.concern: All e2e tests run in Docker via Prefect. The MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=1 escape hatch is NEVER used in e2e tests. Only unit tests may use it.
All e2e tests use MINIVESS_DEBUG_SUFFIX=_E2E_TEST to isolate test experiments from production and debug runs. Cleanup fixture removes _E2E_TEST experiments after the test session.

VesselNN is excluded from all external dataset tests due to potential data leakage with MiniVess (PI authored VesselNN, volumes may overlap). Only DeepVess and TubeNet 2PM are used as external test datasets.
val_compound_masd_cldice = 0.5*(1 - MASD/50) + 0.5*clDice This compound metric from MetricsReloaded balances topology (clDice) and boundary accuracy (MASD). Used for champion tagging and deployment.

Synthetic drift simulation (data drift, model drift, concept drift) is deferred to issue #574 (P0). Phase 3 only verifies the monitoring infrastructure (Evidently, Prometheus, Grafana) works correctly.
All log parsing uses json.loads() on JSONL lines (CLAUDE.md Rule #16). No regex patterns for parsing structured data anywhere in tests.
All docker compose invocations use --env-file to explicitly load .env from the repo root (CLAUDE.md / deployment/CLAUDE.md requirement).

group.name: fix-interflow-seam
group.title: fix: broken train/post-training/analysis inter-flow seam
group.description: Fix the core pipeline seam: train flow never logs checkpoint_dir tags to MLflow, causing post-training and analysis to operate on empty data. Prerequisite for all e2e testing.

group.name: structured-logging
group.title: feat: structured JSONL logging for flow/task transitions
group.description: Add structured JSONL event logging for all flow lifecycle events. Enables container log verification in e2e tests and Prefect UI observability.

group.name: e2e-flow-testing
group.title: test: per-flow e2e smoke tests for all 8 pipeline flows
group.description: Individual flow tests verifying each flow produces correct outputs when run in Docker with real data. Covers acquisition through deploy.

group.name: integration-contracts
group.title: test: inter-flow MLflow/Prefect/volume integration contracts
group.description: Triple verification of inter-flow communication: MLflow artifacts in MinIO, Prefect API flow statuses, container log JSONL events, volume mount contents.

group.name: monitoring-serving
group.title: feat: BentoML calibrated serving + Grafana/Evidently/Prometheus monitoring
group.description: Production monitoring stack: BentoML with calibrated responses, Grafana provisioned dashboards, Evidently drift reports, Prometheus metrics.

group.name: e2e-harness
group.title: test: pytest-docker e2e harness with make test-e2e target
group.description: pytest-docker integration, conftest fixtures, full pipeline orchestration test file, and Makefile target for single-command e2e execution.

group.name: documentation
group.title: docs: update issue #366 with external GPU verification plan
group.description: Update the existing GitHub issue for VesselFM + SAM3 TopoLoRA with cross-references to this e2e plan and drift issue #574.

step.rationale: These tasks have no upstream dependencies and can start immediately. T0.1 (checkpoint tags) is the critical path. T0.5 (debug suffix), T0.6 (JSONL logging), T4.1 (pytest-docker), T4.4 (issue update), and T3.2 (Grafana dashboards) are independent.
T0.2 depends on T0.1 (checkpoint tags exist to read). T0.4 depends on T0.1 (experiment name resolution). T4.5 depends on T4.1 (pytest-docker configured).
T0.3 depends on T0.1 + T0.2 (post-training needs both checkpoint tagging and FlowContract reading to work together).
Acquisition and annotation flows are independent. T1.1 depends on T0.6 (JSONL logging). T1.3 depends on T0.6 (JSONL logging).
Data flow depends on acquisition flow (T1.1) for downloaded data.
Training flow depends on T0.1, T0.4, T0.5, T0.6, T1.2. This is the longest-running step (~30 min).
Post-training depends on T0.3 (checkpoint consumption) and T1.4 (training results).
Analysis depends on T0.4 (experiment name resolution) and T1.5 (post-training variants).
Biostatistics and deploy can run in parallel. Both depend on T1.6 (analysis results).
All integration contract tests can run in parallel after the pipeline completes. They all depend on T1.8 (deploy flow, the last pipeline step).
BentoML inference test depends on T1.8 (deploy flow serving the model).
Evidently and Prometheus tests depend on T3.1 (inference results). T3.4 also depends on T3.2 (Grafana dashboards).
Full pipeline test and Makefile target depend on all previous work. T4.2 orchestrates everything. T4.3 adds the make target.
